The video covers Cherny's system for deciding what becomes a skill and three expansion strategies: agents for delegation, hooks for event-driven automation, and loops with scheduling for continuous execution.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 7 - Pokémon
videos.summary: CS50 2D Lecture 7 covers core mechanics and programming concepts behind Pokémon-inspired RPGs, including state stacks, GUI design, and turn-based battle systems. The lecture is part of Harvard's introduction to computer science and the art of programming, available on edX, Harvard Extension School, and Harvard Summer School.

videos.title: This 26 y/o 10x'd Revenue Doing the Opposite of Every AI Startup | Paraform, John Kim
videos.summary: John Kim built Paraform by positioning human recruiters as the core competitive advantage in hiring, contrary to industry trends toward automation. The company has paid out over $50 million to recruiters and achieved 10x revenue growth in 2025 while serving customers like Palantir and Rippling.

videos.title: My Simple Claude Cowork System (for normal people)
videos.summary: Jeff Su presents Claude Cowork, a system that transforms Claude from a chatbot into a full operating system for work. The approach includes CLAUDE.md instruction files, persistent memory, a three-level hierarchy of workstations and projects, and practical demonstrations like Email HQ and spending trackers.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Cowork (Step-by-Step) | AI Agent on Your Computer
videos.summary: Claude Cowork is an AI agent that runs on desktop computers and automates tasks by interacting with files, emails, calendars, and other applications. The tutorial demonstrates organizing files, scheduling tasks, and integrating with Gmail and Google Calendar through connectors.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 6 - Angry Birds
videos.summary: CS50 2D Lecture 6 covers how Box2D physics engine powers Angry Birds-style gameplay through dynamic bodies, collision detection, joints, and trajectory simulation. The lecture uses Love2D framework to demonstrate game development concepts for mobile games.

videos.title: Claude Design Basics | Master 95% in 10 Minutes
videos.summary: Tristen O'Brien presents a 6-step framework for using Claude Design, Anthropic's new design tool. The video demonstrates building landing pages, pitch decks, and mobile app prototypes from text prompts without coding or design experience, covering interface basics through export and handoff to Claude Code.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 5 - Legend of Zelda
videos.summary: CS50 2D Lecture 5 teaches recreation of Legend of Zelda game mechanics including dungeon generation, hitbox design, screen scrolling, and data-driven game development. The lecture is part of Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming, available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare.

videos.title: The most insane week in AI
videos.summary: Video overview of recent AI developments across multiple domains including new model releases from DeepSeek, OpenAI, Kimi, Xiaomi, and Alibaba Qwen, alongside research projects in game engines, geometry understanding, image generation, and video editing. The content emphasizes rapid innovation velocity and breadth of AI applications from language models to robotics.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 4 - Super Mario Bros.
videos.summary: CS50 2D Lecture 4 teaches game development fundamentals using Lua to build a Super Mario-style platformer. The lecture covers tile maps, animations, procedural level generation, collision detection, entities, and game objects.

videos.title: Claude Design Tutorial: Build a Website in Minutes with AI
videos.summary: Claude Design is a feature by Anthropic that enables users to create website prototypes, presentations, and landing pages from natural language prompts. The tool automatically generates UI, layouts, components, and downloadable code for responsive web pages.

videos.title: GPT-5.5 is a total freak
videos.summary: The AI Search channel presents a review of OpenAI's GPT-5.5, claiming it is exceptional. The video demonstrates the model's capabilities across diverse tasks including code generation, 3D rendering, cancer detection, music composition, and deep research, while also addressing hallucination issues and providing performance benchmarks.

videos.title: SQL Full Course - Build a Database and Deploy It
videos.summary: An AWS tutorial video covering foundational SQL concepts, database design, and hands-on deployment to the cloud. Covers CRUD operations, normalization, relationships, JOINs, and deploying MySQL databases to Amazon RDS.

videos.title: How to 10x Your Claude Code Projects (Karpathy's Method)
videos.summary: Austin Marchese describes three strategies attributed to Andrej Karpathy for improving productivity with Claude Code: LLM Knowledge Bases, Auto-Research, and Context Engineering. The video frames these as a system that can "10x" code project output and positions them as actionable principles for developers using Claude.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 3 - Match 3
videos.summary: CS50 2D Lecture 3 covers building a Match-3 game using tile grids, tweening, timers, and color palettes. The lecture is part of Harvard's CS50 2D course available on edX and Harvard Extension School.

videos.title: Most AI agents are fake (here's a 10-second test)
videos.summary: Jeff Su argues that most companies falsely label products as AI agents when they perform only classification or basic generation. He proposes a simple test: genuine agents autonomously think and iterate, while non-agentic AI merely outputs classifications, numbers, or generated text without autonomous behavior. He uses Google and Anthropic products to illustrate the distinction.

videos.title: Search Relevance Built Into the Vector Index
videos.summary: Qdrant released Query API 1.17.0 with built-in relevance feedback mechanisms for vector search. The feature enables injecting relevance signals directly into the vector index and building adaptive, feedback-driven retrieval systems beyond reranking. The implementation is demonstrated via tutorial for semantic search over documentation.

videos.title: Voice AI that transcribes and takes action
videos.summary: Keith AI presents a voice AI agent that transcribes spoken input and performs corresponding actions. The agent uses function calling to execute user requests.

videos.title: How to Make $20,000/Month with Claude Code
videos.summary: Liam Ottley argues that retainer-based AI agency models for small and medium-sized businesses are now viable and profitable due to Claude Code, enabling recurring monthly revenue starting at $2,500 and scaling to $20,000+. The model involves deploying AI Operating Systems on-site and providing ongoing management and optimization services.

videos.title: Using Hermes to automate social media posts
videos.summary: Keith AI demonstrates using Hermes to automate and systematize social media posts. The video focuses on building repeatable processes that improve post quality rather than relying on guesswork.

videos.title: Biomedical AI Copilot with Neo4j and Qdrant
videos.summary: A biomedical AI copilot demonstration integrating Neo4j graph database and Qdrant vector search to navigate PubMed literature. The system uses context engineering techniques to wire retrieval-augmented generation into an agentic system for medical research applications.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 2 - Breakout
videos.summary: CS50 2D Lecture 2 teaches classic Breakout game development using sprite sheets, collision detection, procedural level generation, particle systems, game states, and persistent high scores. The course is offered through Harvard University via edX, Harvard Extension School, and Harvard Summer School.

videos.title: Use One AI as the CEO, Another as the Worker
videos.summary: A technique for multi-agent AI systems where one agent assumes a management or coordinator role (CEO) while another executes tasks (Worker). This hierarchical approach enables task delegation and specialized role-based operations in AI agent architectures.

videos.title: Best LLM model for Hermes
videos.summary: Keith AI discusses token cost implications of using Claude with Hermes, highlighting that token optimization matters significantly for cost management. The video emphasizes how LLM model selection impacts operational expenses.

videos.title: Turn Any Workflow Into a One-Step “Skill”
videos.summary: Keith AI presents a method for abstracting complex workflows into single-step callable skills. This approach enables automation and reusability of multi-step processes as atomic operations.

videos.title: Qdrant Agent Skills - Discord Session
videos.summary: Qdrant Agent Skills is a new feature that encodes real-world engineering knowledge to help AI agents optimize vector search retrieval and understand when and how to tune performance. The feature moves beyond treating vector search as a black box to enable better search quality and decision-making in production AI systems.

videos.title: Meet Kimi K2.6 Agent Swarm 🐝
videos.summary: Kimi K2.6 introduces Agent Swarm, a system that coordinates up to 300 sub-agents across 4,000 coordinated steps. The system positions multi-agent orchestration as an advancement in test-time compute efficiency.

videos.title: From App Idea to Deployed Infrastructure in minutes
videos.summary: Kiro is an infrastructure automation tool that converts natural language app descriptions into production-ready cloud infrastructure. It handles stack selection, Terraform generation, deployment, observability, and cost estimation through agentic conversation without requiring cloud expertise.

videos.title: Kimi K2.6 and Agent Swarm explained..
videos.summary: Kimi K2.6 is a large language model released by MoonShot AI that improves on K2.5 with better coding capabilities, especially for long-running tasks and multi-agent coordination. The model demonstrates increasing maturity and capability from Chinese AI laboratories.

videos.title: Master Hermes Agent in 41 mins
videos.summary: Keith AI demonstrates how to build a practical personal AI system using Hermes by integrating it with Telegram, Gmail, Google Calendar, Apple Health, and social media. The video covers local and cloud deployment options, skills configuration, token cost optimization via OpenRouter, and comparison with OpenClaw.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 1 - Flappy Bird
videos.summary: CS50 2D Lecture 1 covers the implementation of Flappy Bird using sprites, parallax scrolling, procedural generation, collision detection, state machines, and input handling. The lecture is part of Harvard University's CS50 introduction to computer science, available on edX and through Harvard Extension and Summer Schools.

videos.title: New AI image generator BEATS EVERYTHING
videos.summary: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is an AI image generator from OpenAI that handles a wide range of visual tasks including poster design, UI mockups, sprite sheets, and complex spatial reasoning. The tool demonstrates capabilities in text rendering, animation frame generation, diagram creation, and scene composition at scale.

videos.title: This Ex-Quant Had to Quit Wall Street to Build a 24/7 Stock Exchange | QFEX, Annanay Kapila
videos.summary: Annanay Kapila, an ex-quantitative trader who managed $10 billion daily, left Wall Street to build QFEX, a 24/7 global stock exchange. He critiques high-frequency trading as value-destructive and describes the financial sector as extracting top talent through high compensation rather than creating meaningful work.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 0 - Pong
videos.summary: CS50 2D is Harvard's introductory course on 2D game development fundamentals using Lua and Love2D. The first lecture covers Pong, teaching game loops, input handling, collision detection, object-oriented programming, game states, and sound integration.

videos.title: How Anthropic Employees ACTUALLY Use Claude Code to Grow
videos.summary: Austin Marchese analyzes how Anthropic's Growth Team used Claude Code to achieve rapid scaling from $1 billion to $19 billion in 14 months. He identifies four strategies: automating existing workflows, enabling previously uneconomical work, building unique tools, and using AI as a thought partner, while highlighting a common ceiling where AI becomes busywork rather than leverage.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason presents a video overview of AI agent capabilities and tools. The content covers autoresearch, Claude Code's memory architecture, Hermes self-evolution mechanisms, and Openclaw skill comparison. The title suggests enthusiasm about recent agent developments.

videos.title: Why Inference is hard..
videos.summary: Video tutorial on efficient inference for large language models, covering model loading techniques like mmap and quantization methods including standard quantization, GGUF, AWQ, EXL2, FP8, and NVFP4. Discusses inference engines and optimization strategies for pre-filling, decoding, and concurrent serving.

videos.title: Meet Kimi K2.6: Advancing Open-Source Coding
videos.summary: Kimi AI announced Kimi K2.6, an advancement in open-source coding capabilities. The release emphasizes long-horizon coding tasks, coding-driven design approaches, and agent swarm functionality.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Introduction
videos.summary: CS50 2D is Harvard's introduction to 2D game development that explores the design and mechanics of classic games including Pong, Flappy Bird, Breakout, Super Mario Bros., and others. The course is available through edX, Harvard Extension School, Harvard Summer School, and OpenCourseWare platforms.

videos.title: The 6 Claude Features to Get You Ahead of 99% of People using AI
videos.summary: Tutorial covering six Claude products and features including Claude.ai, desktop app, mobile interface, and API access for building AI agents. Highlights privacy controls and data handling. Emphasizes that most users leverage only a fraction of Claude's full capabilities across writing, thinking, research, and task automation.

videos.title: Stop perfecting your prompt. Do this instead
videos.summary: Jeff Su argues that practitioners should shift focus from prompt perfection to context engineering. He contrasts grounding and RAG as techniques that anchor AI outputs to real data, and emphasizes assembling comprehensive context over optimizing wording.

videos.title: TanStack Start Full Course 2026 | Build and Deploy a Full Stack Application
videos.summary: A full-course tutorial on TanStack Start, a framework addressing hydration errors and RSC directive complexity. The course covers routing, data fetching, server functions, API routes, SEO best practices, and includes a complete full-stack marketplace application (Skild) built from scratch to deployment.

videos.title: keeping up with AI is making you fall behind (just focus on this one thing)
videos.summary: Liam Ottley argues that keeping up with AI news creates anxiety and distraction. He recommends niching down rather than chasing every release, using pattern thinking to spot trends, and focusing on AI Operating Systems as a major market opportunity.

videos.title: STOP Using Claude to Code. Do THIS Instead.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese argues that Claude Code is not just for coding but should form the foundation of a personal AI operating system that learns user patterns, retains memory, and improves with use. He frames this as "owning" AI rather than "renting" it and positions this approach as critical for building sustainable AI-driven workflows and scaling to teams.

videos.title: Claude Code Leak: What's Happening?
videos.summary: A leaked NPM source map revealed Claude Code's internal architecture, showing it is a TypeScript harness wrapping an LLM with a 3-layer memory system. The video analyzes implications for token economics, copyright liability, and privacy telemetry, arguing that open-source alternatives reduce vendor lock-in for individual builders.

videos.title: AI Coding Tools Were Lying to You
videos.summary: Kiro CLI is a code intelligence tool that uses Language Server Protocol (LSP) to enable AI-assisted code editing with semantic understanding. It provides symbol lookup, AST-based pattern search, error detection, and safe refactoring capabilities.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

videos.title: How are you using steering, sub agents and skills?
videos.summary: Kiro explores best practices for guiding AI agents through steering docs, reusable skills, and sub-agent delegation. The video addresses when to use each approach and warns against overusing sub-agents as capabilities increase.

videos.title: Claude Routines Just Dropped, And It's Perfect
videos.summary: Claude Routines is a new feature that allows users to schedule, set up webhooks, and trigger cloud AI agent flows via API calls. The feature enables automation of tasks like email drafting and proposal generation, integrating with workflow platforms like n8n.

videos.title: What’s new in Gemma 4?
videos.summary: Gemma 4 is a new family of open models from Google DeepMind supporting advanced reasoning, native vision and audio, and agentic tool-use capabilities. The models are designed to run across a wide range of hardware from high-end workstations to mobile phones.

videos.title: how to sell $5,000 Claude Code AIOS to SMBs (full breakdown)
videos.summary: Liam Ottley and Tyler Nelson outline a bottom-up playbook for AI agencies to build and sell AI operating systems (AIOS) to small and medium-sized businesses. The approach involves creating a Context OS that integrates chatbot history and external tools like CRM and analytics, layering automations and agents, and implementing ROI tracking before productizing as turnkey solutions or managed services.

videos.title: The physics behind Flow Matching models
videos.summary: Julia Turc provides an in-depth analysis of the Flow Matching training algorithm, covering the physics foundations including time-variant probability density, continuity equations, velocity fields, and optimal transport. The video examines Flow Matching alongside concurrent related works including Rectified Flow and Stochastic Interpolants, with detailed treatment of conditional velocity fields and the algorithmic implementation.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Install Claude Code on Mac (EASY)
videos.summary: A tutorial video on installing Claude Code on macOS. Claude Code is presented as a powerful AI coding assistant that provides fine-grained privacy controls over file and folder access, with installation taking less than 2 minutes on Mac.

videos.title: Why You're Still Better Than AI at Editing Documents - CS50 Tech Talk
videos.summary: AI struggles reliably to edit complex Word documents because their internal structure operates like software with its own logic and rules, not like plain text files. Humans excel at single-document editing while maintaining coherence, whereas computers can scale to thousands of documents only if every change is verifiable and trusted. The speaker argues that treating documents as software rather than files is necessary for reliable AI document editing at scale.

videos.title: Most people get these 3 AI terms wrong
videos.summary: Jeff Su argues that AI hallucination is the same mechanism enabling creativity versus inaccuracy, that context window acts like a fixed-size whiteboard with information dropout, and that tokens are discrete chunks of text with associated costs that explain usage limits.

videos.title: Claude Mythos explained..
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Mythos, which the author characterizes as a frontier model delivering significant performance gains comparable to o1. The author expresses concern about token disparity, where advanced capabilities are privatized to certain companies, potentially limiting broader AI access as intelligence grows.

videos.title: Teaching the foundations of AI in the classroom
videos.summary: Experience AI is a free educational program from Google DeepMind and the Raspberry Pi Foundation designed to teach AI and machine learning foundations to school-aged students. It provides lessons, videos, worksheets, and teacher training using research-backed pedagogies and has reached educators in over 165 countries.

videos.title: Claude Managed Agents Just Dropped, And It Kills n8n
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Managed Agents, a platform designed to replace no-code automation builders like n8n. The service enables users to build autonomous agents without API keys, with features including built-in debugging, session management, environment vaults, security controls, and cost analytics.

videos.title: 5 Claude Cowork Tricks (in 10 min)
videos.summary: A YouTube video tutorial by Tristen O'Brien demonstrates five practical uses of Claude Cowork, an AI tool by Anthropic, for automating business tasks. The video shows how Claude Cowork can reduce a 10-hour daily administrative workload to a streamlined process by analyzing financial spreadsheets, comparing supplier quotes, and generating business reports for small businesses.

videos.title: Claude Mythos Preview: Everything You Need to Know
videos.summary: Nick Saraev reviews Claude Mythos, questioning whether Anthropic released a model "too good" that may signal the end of open Claude models and mark a shift toward a next generation of closed models. The video suggests Mythos demonstrates capabilities that could reshape the landscape of accessible AI models.

videos.title: Learn 80% of Claude Cowork in Under 20 Minutes
videos.summary: Claude Cowork is Anthropic's desktop app that extends Claude from a chatbot into a full productivity system. It enables local file access, persistent memory, connectors, skills, projects, browser extension, and scheduled tasks for automating workflows like expense reports and inbox triage.

videos.title: Mission Report Ep. 03 | Antigravity Updates
videos.summary: Google Antigravity is an agentic development platform that evolves the IDE into the agent-first era. It enables developers to operate at a task-oriented level by managing agents across workspaces while retaining a familiar AI IDE experience. Agents operate across editor, terminal, and browser to autonomously plan and execute complex end-to-end tasks.

videos.title: AutoResearch explained..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains AutoResearch from Andrej Karpathy as an early approach to iterative self-improvement in software development. The video discusses how AutoResearch offers a novel perspective on improving software, though notes the approach is not universally applicable.

videos.title: Introducing Cursor 3
videos.summary: Cursor released version 3, positioning it as a simpler and more powerful development environment designed for a future where code generation by AI agents is standard practice. The tool maintains the full depth of a development environment while being optimized for agent-based code authoring.

videos.title: #claude Chat vs. Cowork: Biggest 3 differences
videos.summary: Claude Cowork is positioned as an advanced variant of Claude Chat with three key improvements: direct file access from the user's computer without upload limits or file size caps, a larger context window that preserves conversation details longer, and integrated output generation that produces ready-to-use files directly in folders rather than chat-based responses.

videos.title: TurboQuant Explained..
videos.summary: TurboQuant is a Google quantization technique that compresses KV cache and vectors to reduce VRAM and graphics card requirements. It uses vector quantization with codebooks and optimizes for mean squared error and inner product error.

videos.title: I Stopped Typing to OpenClaw — Typeless voice dictation
videos.summary: Typeless is an AI voice dictation tool that enables users to compose and control applications via speech input at significantly higher speeds than typing. The tool supports multilingual content creation, AI-assisted transcription, and integration with other applications through voice-controlled actions.

videos.title: CS50 Outtakes 2013
videos.summary: CS50 Outtakes 2013 is a compilation of out-of-take moments from Harvard's CS50 computer science course. The content provides informal, unscripted commentary on computer science education and instruction.

videos.title: Arm AGI CPU explained..
videos.summary: Arm announced its first AGI CPU targeting agentic workloads, entering a highly competitive CPU market. The market is capital-intensive with high time-to-market risk as hyperscalers increasingly customize CPUs for their specific data center architectures, working with TSMC on supply chain management.

videos.title: How to Use Gamma AI to Make Stunning Presentations
videos.summary: Gamma AI is an AI-powered presentation tool that generates complete presentations from a single prompt, document, or URL in minutes. It automates research, structure, design, and revisions while offering editing via built-in AI agents, presentation modes, and export to PowerPoint, Google Slides, and PDF. The tool includes API capabilities for enterprise automation in sales, marketing, training, and reporting workflows.

videos.title: I Built an AI That Builds You a Course on Anything (In Seconds)
videos.summary: Tristen O'Brien built an AI tool that generates personalized online courses based on user-specified topics and skill levels in under two minutes. The tool structures learning pathways using Claude, organizes content into syllabi, and includes an AI chatbot tutor for course interaction.

videos.title: How to Build Professional Video Animations with Claude.
videos.summary: Tutorial on using Claude Code and Remotion to generate professional video animations from text prompts. Covers installation, visual style configuration, plan mode workflow, and end-to-end animation creation in approximately ten minutes without manual editing.

videos.title: Paperclip Sucks, Actually
videos.summary: Nick Saraev argues that Paperclip is overhyped and encourages looking for better alternatives. He positions himself as someone with practical real-world automation experience, having built automated businesses generating seven-figure revenue over six years.

videos.title: CLAUDE CODE ADVANCED FULL COURSE (3 HOURS)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev teaches an advanced 3-hour course on Claude Code covering system prompts optimization, agent harnesses, multi-agent teams, subagents, auto-research workflows, browser automation, and workspace organization for building larger AI projects.

videos.title: Claude Cowork: The #1 Thing To Know
videos.summary: Claude Cowork differs from Claude Chat in prompting style: Chat requires task-first, step-by-step guidance, while Cowork uses outcome-first language where users describe end goals and let the system determine approach. Example shows Cowork completes photo renaming and sorting autonomously versus Chat providing recommendations for manual execution.

videos.title: OpenClaw Sub Agents and ACPX (OpenClaw Business Automation Setup)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates OpenClaw as a business automation platform deployed on commodity hardware running AI agents to manage websites, learn brand guidelines, and orchestrate sub-agents in content pipelines. The video compares multi-agent orchestration using OpenClaw with single-agent workflows using Claude Code.

videos.title: Introducing Lyria 3 Pro
videos.summary: Google DeepMind introduced Lyria 3 Pro, an advanced music generation model that enables creation of custom tracks up to 3 minutes long with enhanced customization and creative control. The tool is being integrated into more Google products following the earlier release of Lyria 3.

videos.title: Claude Computer Use Just Dropped, Here's How to Hack It
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates practical applications of Claude's computer use feature for automation tasks including LinkedIn outreach, data scraping, form submission, ad management, and QA testing. The video emphasizes real-world revenue-generating use cases and acknowledges some approaches that bypass standard browser features.

videos.title: Qdrant Multi-Vector Search Course Overview
videos.summary: Qdrant has launched a Multi-Vector Search Course available at their course portal. The course covers multi-vector search capabilities, a technique for retrieving information across multiple vector representations simultaneously.

videos.title: Late Interaction Basics | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Late interaction is a search paradigm that preserves token-level representations while enabling pre-computation of documents, contrasted against single-vector embeddings and cross-encoders. The video explains how late interaction models like ColBERT balance retrieval speed with relevance precision for large-scale collections.

videos.title: Use Cases for Multi-Vector Search | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Lesson comparing single-vector and multi-vector search approaches using BGE versus ColBERT on technical support queries. Multi-vector search with token-level verification prevents false positives from keyword-stuffed content by requiring strong matches at the token level rather than just topical overlap.

videos.title: Problems of Multi-Vector Search | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Multi-vector search improves retrieval quality but requires brute-force scanning because HNSW indexing fails with MaxSim due to its asymmetric, query-dependent nature. Storing hundreds of token embeddings per document incurs roughly 167x more memory overhead than single-vector approaches, creating significant computational and storage costs at scale.

videos.title: Multi-Vector Embeddings in Qdrant | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Tutorial on configuring Qdrant for multi-vector search using the MaxSim comparator, indexing documents with FastEmbed local inference, and querying with ColBERT embeddings. Covers collection setup, HNSW disabling for MaxSim compatibility, and on-disk vector storage for memory-constrained environments.

videos.title: How ColPali Models Work | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: ColPali extends late interaction from text to visual documents by using a Vision Language Model (PaliGemma-3B) to divide document images into a 32x32 grid of patches, each producing visual token embeddings. Text queries match directly against these visual patches using MaxSim scoring without requiring OCR.

videos.title: ColPali Family Overview | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: The ColPali family includes compact embedding models (ColSmol, ColFlor, ColModernVBERT) for visual document retrieval with reduced parameters compared to the base ColPali. Multilingual variants from NVIDIA and Nomic AI are available, and ViDoRe V3 serves as the benchmark for evaluating visual retrieval models. Qdrant is a vector search engine in Rust supporting semantic and hybrid search.

videos.title: Visual Interpretability of ColPali | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Qdrant demonstrates visual interpretability for ColPali embeddings, which represent documents as 1,024 patch embeddings mapped to spatial locations. The system computes per-token similarity heatmaps overlaid on documents to show which regions match query terms, enabling interpretable multi-vector search and relevance debugging.

videos.title: Multi-Stage Retrieval with Universal Query API | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Qdrant's prefetch plus rerank pattern uses fast initial retrieval (single-vector or BM25) to narrow candidates down from millions to hundreds, then applies ColBERT reranking to refine results. The technique emphasizes oversampling in stage one to ensure sufficient candidates for effective reranking.

videos.title: CS50 for Business - Lecture 8 - Securing Systems
videos.summary: CS50 for Business Lecture 8 covers securing systems through strong passwords, encryption, two-factor authentication, and cybersecurity best practices. The lecture is part of Harvard University's introduction to computer science and available through multiple platforms including edX and OpenCourseWare. The content emphasizes data protection strategies against cyber threats and hackers.

videos.title: How (and why) to take a logarithm of an image
videos.summary: 3Blue1Brown explores how logarithmic transformations can be applied to images, using Escher's Print Gallery as a case study. The video demonstrates connections between complex analysis, conformal maps, and artistic visualization through animated mathematical exposition.

videos.title: How to Build & Sell AI Agents in 2026: Ultimate Beginner’s Guide
videos.summary: A comprehensive tutorial on building and selling AI agents using n8n without coding experience. The guide covers four real-world AI agents including a Telegram expense tracker, website lead generation chatbot, voice agent with Retell AI, and a sales copilot with Lovable frontend, along with monetization strategies for launching an AI automation agency.

videos.title: Choosing the Wrong Language Costs AI Teams Months of Work
videos.summary: Darren Builds AI argues that programming language choice significantly impacts AI development team productivity and that static typing via TypeScript, combined with LSP tooling and compile-time checks, reduces friction compared to dynamic languages like Python. The speaker emphasizes that error detection mechanisms become more critical as teams scale.

videos.title: CS50 for Business - Lecture 7 - Deploying Databases
videos.summary: CS50 for Business Lecture 7 covers deploying and managing databases at scale using SQL and noSQL. The lecture teaches best practices for efficient data storage and querying. It is part of CS50, Harvard University's computer science course available through edX, Harvard Extension School, and other platforms.

videos.title: If You Use Agent Zero, This Video is for You (EPIC FAIL)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs documents a failed attempt to build an AI ad campaign generator using Agent Zero, encountering persistent LiteLLM errors that made the framework unusable. He concludes that Agent Zero shows promise but advises testing thoroughly before committing production workflows, noting Claude Code or OpenClaw would have completed the task.

videos.title: How We Scaled Kimi K2.5 | Zhilin Yang's full GTC 2026 Keynote
videos.summary: Zhilin Yang presented Kimi AI's approach to scaling the Kimi K2.5 model at GTC 2026. The keynote covers technical decisions and methodology behind achieving the model's performance at scale.

videos.title: Claude Channels Just Dropped, And It Kills OpenClaw (Again)
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Channels, a new feature that encapsulates functionality previously available in OpenClaw. The feature allows users to set up integrated workflows across Discord, Telegram, and other platforms within Claude Code, demonstrated with setup guidance across multiple communication channels.

videos.title: This NotebookLM feature cuts my workload by 50%
videos.summary: Jeff Su demonstrates NotebookLM's mind map feature, which generates visual topic hierarchies from uploaded documents. Users can selectively explore branches of interest rather than reviewing all source material, reducing preparation workload for content projects.

videos.title: COLD EMAIL COPYWRITING & OUTREACH Full Course 2026
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's "Cold Email Copywriting & Outreach Full Course 2026" is a multi-hour educational program covering the psychology of persuasion, copywriting frameworks, offer construction, platform-specific optimization (LinkedIn, X, SMS, Instagram), email subject lines, follow-ups, and AI integration. The course includes real-world cold email analysis and advanced outbound techniques.

videos.title: Claude Dispatch Just Dropped, And It Kills OpenClaw
videos.summary: Claude Dispatch is Anthropic's new feature for agentic workflows through the Claude app. It provides a more secure alternative to OpenClaw by running autonomous tasks directly within Claude rather than through third-party platforms like Telegram.

videos.title: I Set Up a Claude Code Operating System for 6 Founders in 8 Hours
videos.summary: Liam Ottley demonstrates setting up Claude Code as an AI Operating System for six non-technical founders in a single day. The setup includes a Context OS with four context files teaching Claude about business operations, enabling non-coders to build automation for invoicing, finance reporting, competitor analysis, and content workflows.

videos.title: NotebookLM Changed Completely: Here's What Matters (in 2026)
videos.summary: Jeff Su reviews NotebookLM's major 2026 updates including a three-column layout and Studio panel tools. He ranks tools into Tier 1 (Reports, Slide Decks, Infographics, Mind Maps) and Tier 2 (Data Tables, Video Overviews, Quiz, Flashcards, Audio Overviews) categories. He demonstrates workflows for turning source documents into finished reports, presentations, and social-ready content.
